texts.label: text
texts.orig: ii	Clinical Reminder Setup Guide	March 2009
texts.text: ii	Clinical Reminder Setup Guide	March 2009

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The National Defense and Authorization Act (NDAA) calls for VA Medical Centers to use standardized documentation that outlines the plan of rehabilitation care and community reintegration for patients who have experienced a Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI). To meet the needs of the NDAA, Patient Care Services (PCS) supports the development of a standardized progress note title and associated documentation method for TBI Rehabilitation Care and Community Reintegration for use in the Computerized Patient Record System or in Veterans Health Information Systems & Technology Architecture (VistA) Order Entry/Results Reporting (OE/RR).
texts.text: The National Defense and Authorization Act (NDAA) calls for VA Medical Centers to use standardized documentation that outlines the plan of rehabilitation care and community reintegration for patients who have experienced a Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI). To meet the needs of the NDAA, Patient Care Services (PCS) supports the development of a standardized progress note title and associated documentation method for TBI Rehabilitation Care and Community Reintegration for use in the Computerized Patient Record System or in Veterans Health Information Systems & Technology Architecture (VistA) Order Entry/Results Reporting (OE/RR).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Ultimately, the addition of a standardized note title and documentation will ensure all patients receive the same level of care. It will also allow for national monitoring of the program as standardized data can be rolled up and extracted to those individuals who monitor the program directly.
texts.text: Ultimately, the addition of a standardized note title and documentation will ensure all patients receive the same level of care. It will also allow for national monitoring of the program as standardized data can be rolled up and extracted to those individuals who monitor the program directly.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This project consists of patch numbers PXRM*2.0*15 and TIU*1*246.
texts.text: This project consists of patch numbers PXRM*2.0*15 and TIU*1*246.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Reminder patch installs the Reminder Dialog VA-TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHAB/ REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE on your local system. The dialog will be used by Rehab clinicians to document the interdisciplinary treatment plan of care. The clinician will document the Type of Plan: an Initial plan, Interim Plan, or Discharge plan. The Type of Plan will be stored as a PCE Health Factor. In a future patch, the type of plan will be used to trigger a reminder for a follow-up review of the plan.
texts.text: The Reminder patch installs the Reminder Dialog VA-TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHAB/ REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE on your local system. The dialog will be used by Rehab clinicians to document the interdisciplinary treatment plan of care. The clinician will document the Type of Plan: an Initial plan, Interim Plan, or Discharge plan. The Type of Plan will be stored as a PCE Health Factor. In a future patch, the type of plan will be used to trigger a reminder for a follow-up review of the plan.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The remainder of the reminder dialog contains required topics that one clinician will use to collect word processing responses to meet requirements specified by the Office of Rehabilitation Services at VACO and the Secretary’s office.
texts.text: The remainder of the reminder dialog contains required topics that one clinician will use to collect word processing responses to meet requirements specified by the Office of Rehabilitation Services at VACO and the Secretary’s office.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch deploys the TBI/POLYTRAUMA DOCUMENTS / TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHABILITATION/REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE Document Class/Title Pair in support of NSR #20080818. It maps the newly introduced TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHABILITATION/REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE National Title to the TBI
texts.text: This patch deploys the TBI/POLYTRAUMA DOCUMENTS / TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHABILITATION/REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE Document Class/Title Pair in support of NSR #20080818. It maps the newly introduced TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHABILITATION/REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE National Title to the TBI

It will optionally allow the site to attach the new TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHABILITATION/ REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE National Title to a local document class at the Clinical Application Coordinator's discretion.
texts.text: TREATMENT PLAN NOTE Enterprise Standard Title, and if possible, activates the new title. It will optionally allow the site to attach the new TBI/POLYTRAUMA REHABILITATION/ REINTEGRATION PLAN OF CARE National Title to a local document class at the Clinical Application Coordinator's discretion.

texts.orig: Prior to installation, the routine checks to see whether the user is a member of the Clinical Application Coordinator (CAC) User Class. If not, it will prompt the user to affirm that they either have a CAC present with them, or have consulted a CAC, in order to offer an informed
texts.text: Prior to installation, the routine checks to see whether the user is a member of the Clinical Application Coordinator (CAC) User Class. If not, it will prompt the user to affirm that they either have a CAC present with them, or have consulted a CAC, in order to offer an informed

texts.label: text
texts.orig: decision as to which Document Class the new Title should be assigned to (with the new National TBI/POLYTRAUMA DOCUMENTS as default). It will also detect the case where the installation is complete, and if necessary, allow the site to move the Title to a different Document Class, cleaning up any residue in the process.

texts.orig: In CPRS, create the new template and assign the reminder dialog to it. This is done by accessing the template editor from the Notes Tab in CPRS Options|Create New Template.

texts.orig: Text: The sections included in the TBI/Polytrauma Rehabilitation and Reintegration Plan of Care are mandated as per the National Defense and	Authorization Act of FY '08, Section 1702, paragraph 1710C. texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Q: What is meant by Current Problems (physical, mental, spiritually, etc.…)
texts.text: Q: What is meant by Current Problems (physical, mental, spiritually, etc.…)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A: The purpose of the template is to document the Rehabilitation and Reintegration plan of care. Community reintegration refers to the resumption of age, gender, and culturally appropriate roles in the family, community and workplace. Community reintegration should emphasize a multidisciplinary approach, which also includes peers and family, in the attempt to close the gap between treatment activities and functional competence in the individual’s natural environment. The primary focus of community reintegration should be on what the individual with polytrauma and TBI needs to achieve for returning to home, community, work, or school. That being said, the current problems section of the template should be used to address any barriers the patient is facing, which are limiting their ability to successfully resume the roles mentioned above. There is intentional flexibility to allow the treatment team to document the primary problems for each individual patient, but this could refer to physical, mental, spiritual problems, or even be broken down further into memory, balance, strength, cognition, etc., problems.
texts.text: A: The purpose of the template is to document the Rehabilitation and Reintegration plan of care. Community reintegration refers to the resumption of age, gender, and culturally appropriate roles in the family, community and workplace. Community reintegration should emphasize a multidisciplinary approach, which also includes peers and family, in the attempt to close the gap between treatment activities and functional competence in the individual’s natural environment. The primary focus of community reintegration should be on what the individual with polytrauma and TBI needs to achieve for returning to home, community, work, or school. That being said, the current problems section of the template should be used to address any barriers the patient is facing, which are limiting their ability to successfully resume the roles mentioned above. There is intentional flexibility to allow the treatment team to document the primary problems for each individual patient, but this could refer to physical, mental, spiritual problems, or even be broken down further into memory, balance, strength, cognition, etc., problems.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Q: Who is supposed to be the managing physician if the patient is seen by multiple specialists, such as MH, TBI, primary care, and what if the patient is being discharged from the hospital.
texts.text: Q: Who is supposed to be the managing physician if the patient is seen by multiple specialists, such as MH, TBI, primary care, and what if the patient is being discharged from the hospital.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A: The managing physician should be the physiatrist on the Polytrauma/TBI Interdisciplinary Team.
texts.text: A: The managing physician should be the physiatrist on the Polytrauma/TBI Interdisciplinary Team.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Q: Who is the Military Case Manager if the patient is discharged from the military? Does the document need to be sent to the military if the patient is a veteran? This raises the question: why are these fields required?
texts.text: Q: Who is the Military Case Manager if the patient is discharged from the military? Does the document need to be sent to the military if the patient is a veteran? This raises the question: why are these fields required?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A: This would not be applicable if the patient has been discharged from the military. The law says VA is to assume case management roles if they are discharged.
texts.text: A: This would not be applicable if the patient has been discharged from the military. The law says VA is to assume case management roles if they are discharged.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Q: What is the date of review if the patient is being discharged from the hospital? This raises the question: why is this field required?
texts.text: Q: What is the date of review if the patient is being discharged from the hospital? This raises the question: why is this field required?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A: The template is to document the Rehabilitation and Reintegration plan of care for a patient as s/he transitions back into the community. It is not an inpatient-specific tool, but rather a tool for the IDT to document their plan as patients resume their roles or ‘reintegrates’ back into the community.
texts.text: A: The template is to document the Rehabilitation and Reintegration plan of care for a patient as s/he transitions back into the community. It is not an inpatient-specific tool, but rather a tool for the IDT to document their plan as patients resume their roles or ‘reintegrates’ back into the community.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Q: Is the Discharge Plan of Care for being discharged from the hospital or from the program?
texts.text: Q: Is the Discharge Plan of Care for being discharged from the hospital or from the program?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A: This can be either, depending upon the overall plan for the patient. If the IDT feels an inpatient does not have future issues to be addressed related to their rehabilitation and
texts.text: A: This can be either, depending upon the overall plan for the patient. If the IDT feels an inpatient does not have future issues to be addressed related to their rehabilitation and

texts.label: text
texts.orig: reintegration back into the community, it may take place at the time of DC from the hospital. In many cases, these individuals may continue to require services as they resume their roles in the community, and the Discharge plan of care would then be utilized when they are ultimately DC’d from the IDT program while an outpatient.
texts.text: reintegration back into the community, it may take place at the time of DC from the hospital. In many cases, these individuals may continue to require services as they resume their roles in the community, and the Discharge plan of care would then be utilized when they are ultimately DC’d from the IDT program while an outpatient.
